rows.O: Ki67 80%. Neoadjuvant chemotherapy as a priority followed by radical mastectomy.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Triple-negative breast cancer cT4dN1Mx (infiltrating ductal carcinoma grade 3, immunohistochemistry: estrogen receptor negative, progesterone receptor negative, Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 score 0)
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Female patient, 35 years old, resident of Cañas. In April 2023, while breastfeeding her one-year-old baby, she noticed a lump in her breast which she initially attributed to milk. Over subsequent days the lump grew, prompting her physician to recommend an ultrasound and biopsy. She attended Hospital Dr. Enrique Baltodano Briceño, where a biopsy was performed. On 26 June 2023 the diagnosis was confirmed as triple-negative breast cancer cT4dN1Mx with rapid progression. Breast ultrasound (27/06/2023) described a lobulated, heterogeneous mass of 44x42x45 mm with ipsilateral axillary adenopathies. A staging CT scan on 10/07/2023 showed no evidence of metastatic disease. At first consultation the tumor was classified as inflammatory carcinoma: a mass occupying the entire breast, associated with erythema and orange-peel skin, with multiple palpable mobile lymph nodes in the right axilla.
fields.SYMPTOMS: Rapid growth of breast lesion; erythema; orange-peel skin (peau d'orange) appearance of the breast; multiple palpable mobile axillary lymph nodes; patient anxiety and emotional distress
fields.DISEASE_HISTORY: In April 2023 patient noticed a breast lump while breastfeeding. Rapid progression was documented. Biopsy confirmed infiltrating ductal carcinoma grade 3, triple-negative subtype (Ki67 80%). Staging CT showed no metastatic disease. Neoadjuvant chemotherapy with Paclitaxel and Carboplatin was initiated (weekly, 12 cycles), with 7 sessions completed by the time of the amparo filing. Excellent clinical response documented (ECOG Performance Status 0 at follow-up on 24/08/2023). The treating oncologist (Dr. Steffi Patricia Romero Mendez) requested Pembrolizumab as a non-LOM drug on 04/07/2023. The Local Pharmacotherapeutics Committee (Comite Local de Farmacoterapia) forwarded the request to the Central Pharmacotherapeutics Committee on 06/07/2023. The Central Committee denied the request on 19/07/2023 (session 2023-30), stating that the evidence did not demonstrate a benefit in overall survival or quality of life.
fields.FUNCTIONAL_LIMITATIONS: ECOG Performance Status 0 (documented at follow-up on 24/08/2023)
fields.COMORBIDITIES: No comorbidities reported (no known prior conditions documented)
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy with Paclitaxel (160 mg IV weekly) and Carboplatin (AUC 2, 295 mg IV weekly), 12-cycle regimen; 7 cycles completed at time of amparo filing. Pre-medications: Tropisetron, Chlorpheniramine, Cimetidine, Dexamethasone
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND (treatment was ongoing with excellent clinical response; Pembrolizumab was sought as an add-on to ongoing neoadjuvant chemotherapy, not due to failure of current therapy)
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 %: NOT FOUND | Microsatellite Instability status: NOT FOUND | Tumor Mutational Burden: NOT FOUND | Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2: score 0 (negative) | Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor: NOT FOUND | Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase: NOT FOUND | ROS1: NOT FOUND | KRAS: NOT FOUND | B-Raf Proto-Oncogene: NOT FOUND | others: Ki67 80%; estrogen receptor negative; progesterone receptor negative
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: No metastasis documented (staging CT on 10/07/2023 showed no evidence of metastatic disease; cT4dN1Mx — Mx means metastasis not assessed or unknown at staging)
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: The treating oncologist argued that Pembrolizumab, in combination with neoadjuvant chemotherapy (Carboplatin/Paclitaxel followed by anthracyclines), is the new standard of care for locally advanced triple-negative breast cancer. Based on the KEYNOTE-522 trial, the regimen improves the complete pathological response rate and reduces the risk of local or distant recurrence, thereby improving disease-free survival. The patient is young, the tumor is high-risk, and an intent-to-cure approach demands the best available treatment. The forensic medical expert (Dictamen Medico Legal No. 2023-0000870/2023-0000879) confirmed that Pembrolizumab is valid and more beneficial for this patient, citing FDA approval and ASCO guidelines for triple-negative breast cancer stage T2-T4 or T1c N1-2.
fields.URGENCY: High urgency — the treating oncologist stressed that Pembrolizumab should be added as soon as possible to the ongoing neoadjuvant chemotherapy regimen, given the rapid tumor progression and the window of opportunity during neoadjuvant treatment
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: Risk of disease progression and loss of the neoadjuvant treatment window if Pembrolizumab is not added promptly; the tumor had shown rapid progression prior to treatment initiation
fields.RISK_HOSPITALIZATION: NOT FOUND (not explicitly stated in the document)
fields.RISK_DEATH: The treating oncologist and the court noted that the denial of Pembrolizumab jeopardizes the patient's life; the medication is intended to prolong life and improve survival in a young patient with a high-risk, rapidly evolving cancer
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab 25 mg/mL, 200 mg intravenously every 3 weeks, for 17 applications (in combination with neoadjuvant chemotherapy, followed by adjuvant monotherapy after surgery)
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Constitutional Chamber found that the treating oncologist's criterion must prevail when there is a conflict with the Central Pharmacotherapeutics Committee regarding non-LOM medications. The court obtained an independent expert opinion from the Forensic Medical Unit of the Judicial Investigation Agency (OIJ), which concluded that Pembrolizumab is valid and more beneficial for this patient, in accordance with evidence-based medicine, FDA approval, and ASCO recommendations. Based on this impartial expert determination, the court ordered the immediate supply of Pembrolizumab in the dose and duration prescribed by the treating physician, under her strict supervision, so long as no clinical contraindication arises.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Article 71 of the Ley de la Jurisdiccion Constitucional (penalty for non-compliance with amparo orders); Article 12 of the Pacto Internacional de Derechos Economicos, Sociales y Culturales (PIDESC) — right to health; Article 21 of the Constitucion Politica de Costa Rica — right to life; Constitutional Chamber jurisprudence on respecting the treating physician's criterion in conflicts with the Central Pharmacotherapeutics Committee; Dictamen Medico Legal No. 2023-0000870/2023-0000879 (Forensic Medicine Unit, OIJ); KEYNOTE-522 trial (Schmid et al.); FDA approval for triple-negative breast cancer early high-risk stage; ASCO guidelines for triple-negative breast cancer T2-T4 or T1c N1-2; Reglamento sobre Expediente Electronico ante el Poder Judicial (session No. 27-11, 22 August 2011, article XXVI); Consejo Superior del Poder Judicial session No. 43-12, 3 May 2012, article LXXXI

missing_from_no: Patient age (35 years old) is absent from N/O. The Ki67 value (80%) is present in O but specific biomarker details (Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 score 0, estrogen receptor negative, progesterone receptor negative) are not mentioned. The staging CT result (no metastatic disease) is absent. The forensic medical expert finding, the judicial decision outcome, and the ECOG Performance Status 0 are not captured in N/O.
possibly_incorrect: Column N states 'cT4dN1Mx' which matches the document. Column O states 'Ki67 80%. Neoadjuvant chemotherapy as a priority followed by radical mastectomy' — the document confirms Ki67 80% and the proposed plan of neoadjuvant chemotherapy followed by radical mastectomy; no conflicts detected with the document content.

rows.N: Right triple-negative breast cancer cT4b cN2 M0

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Right triple-negative breast cancer, cT4b cN2 M0 (locally advanced, high-risk)
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Female patient, 33 years old, resident of Desamparados, with no known personal medical history. She is a patient at Hospital San Juan de Dios. Clinical examination documented a large mass in the right breast involving the areola-nipple complex, with two bleeding ulcers, and a conglomerate of right axillary adenopathies. A staging CT scan from March 2023 documented metastatic disease. ECOG Performance Status 0. The case was discussed in a multidisciplinary oncology session and the team agreed to request Pembrolizumab in combination with chemotherapy as neoadjuvant treatment, based on the KEYNOTE-522 study, given the patient's young age and high-risk cancer. At the time of the Central Committee's analysis (April 2023), the patient had not yet received neoadjuvant treatment; surgical treatment and radiotherapy were pending.
fields.SYMPTOMS: Large mass in the right breast involving the areola-nipple complex; two bleeding ulcers on the breast; conglomerate of right axillary adenopathies
fields.DISEASE_HISTORY: The patient was diagnosed with right triple-negative breast cancer cT4b cN2 M0. A staging CT in March 2023 documented metastatic disease (noted by the Local Committee report; however the Central Committee and forensic expert describe the staging as M0 — this may reflect a discrepancy in imaging interpretation). At the time of the first medication request (11 April 2023), she had not started neoadjuvant treatment. The treating oncologist (Dr. Hellen Hernandez Steller) at Hospital San Juan de Dios requested Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks for 6 months in combination with neoadjuvant chemotherapy. The Local Pharmacotherapeutics Committee forwarded the request (note HSJD-DG-CLF: 1141-2023, 17 April 2023) to the Central Pharmacotherapeutics Committee, which denied it in session 2023-16 on 26 April 2023 (note GM-CCF-2072-2023, 05 May 2023) on grounds that the evidence did not demonstrate a benefit in overall survival or quality of life. The amparo was filed on 12 June 2023. The Constitutional Chamber requested an independent forensic medical opinion (Dictamen Medico Legal No. 2023-5086, 05 July 2023), which concluded that the patient would benefit from adding Pembrolizumab as soon as possible.
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: None documented prior to the amparo; neoadjuvant chemotherapy was pending at time of filing
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND (no prior oncological treatment had been administered; Pembrolizumab was requested as part of first-line neoadjuvant therapy)
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 %: not documented at the time of the Central Committee review (noted as absent and described as a predictor of response) | Microsatellite Instability status: NOT FOUND | Tumor Mutational Burden: NOT FOUND | Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2: NOT FOUND (triple-negative implies negative, but not explicitly measured in document) | Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor: NOT FOUND | Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase: NOT FOUND | ROS1: NOT FOUND | KRAS: NOT FOUND | B-Raf Proto-Oncogene: NOT FOUND | others: Triple-negative breast cancer (estrogen receptor, progesterone receptor, and Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 negative by definition)
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: Staging CT from March 2023 documented metastatic disease (location not specified in the document; axillary lymph node involvement clinically documented as cN2); the staging designation M0 in the primary diagnosis suggests no confirmed distant metastasis at staging, but the CT finding of metastatic disease introduces ambiguity
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: The treating oncologist recommended Pembrolizumab based on KEYNOTE-522 results showing a significant improvement in complete pathological response rate (64.8% vs 51.2%) with pembrolizumab plus neoadjuvant chemotherapy compared to chemotherapy alone. A higher complete pathological response rate correlates with better long-term outcomes, especially in triple-negative breast cancer. The forensic specialist (Dr. Maria Jose Ibarra Valldeperas) confirmed that for high-risk triple-negative breast cancer (stage II and III), Pembrolizumab added to neoadjuvant chemotherapy increases the probability of complete pathological response and improves long-term results. No Programmed Death-Ligand 1 measurement is required as a criterion for this indication. The patient was in the first 25% of her neoadjuvant chemotherapy when the forensic assessment was made, making prompt addition of Pembrolizumab critical.
fields.URGENCY: High urgency — the forensic expert noted the patient was in the early phase of neoadjuvant chemotherapy and that Pembrolizumab should be added as soon as possible to maximize the chance of achieving complete pathological response before surgery
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: Risk of missing the optimal therapeutic window during neoadjuvant chemotherapy if Pembrolizumab is not added promptly; reduced probability of complete pathological response at surgery with corresponding worse long-term outcomes
fields.RISK_DEATH: Young patient with locally advanced, high-risk triple-negative breast cancer and high recurrence risk; failure to achieve complete pathological response is associated with significantly worse overall survival
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab 200 mg intravenously every 3 weeks for 6 months, in combination with neoadjuvant chemotherapy (per KEYNOTE-522 protocol), followed by adjuvant Pembrolizumab after surgery
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Constitutional Chamber confirmed that the treating physician's criterion must be respected when it conflicts with the Central Pharmacotherapeutics Committee regarding non-LOM drugs. The court obtained an independent forensic medical opinion (Dictamen Medico Legal No. 2023-5086) from the Clinical Medical Forensic Section of the OIJ, which concluded that the patient would benefit from the addition of Pembrolizumab to her chemotherapy regimen as soon as possible, to increase the probability of complete pathological response before surgery and improve long-term outcomes. Based on this impartial expert determination, the court ordered the immediate supply of Pembrolizumab in the prescribed dose and duration, under the strict supervision of the treating physician.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Article 71 of the Ley de la Jurisdiccion Constitucional (penalty for non-compliance with amparo orders); Article 12 of the Pacto Internacional de Derechos Economicos, Sociales y Culturales (PIDESC); Article 21 of the Constitucion Politica; Observacion 14 of the PIDESC Committee on the right to health; Article 16 of the Ley General de la Administracion Publica (prohibition on acts contrary to the rules of science or technique); KEYNOTE-522 trial (Schmid et al., New England Journal of Medicine 2020 and 2022); EMA and FDA product information for Pembrolizumab (Keytruda); Dictamen Medico Legal No. 2023-5086 (OIJ, 05 July 2023); Constitutional Chamber jurisprudence on respecting the treating physician's criterion; Reglamento sobre Expediente Electronico ante el Poder Judicial (Corte Plena session No. 27-11, 22 August 2011, article XXVI); Consejo Superior del Poder Judicial session No. 43-12, 3 May 2012, article LXXXI

missing_from_no: Patient age (33 years old), ECOG Performance Status 0, absence of prior oncological treatments, the staging CT finding of metastatic disease (which conflicts with M0 designation), the Programmed Death-Ligand 1 status as undocumented, the forensic medical expert conclusion, and the judicial decision outcome (granted) are all absent from N/O.
possibly_incorrect: Column N states 'cT4b cN2 M0' which is confirmed in the document. Column O states 'Neoadjuvant treatment' — this is correct but incomplete; the document also specifies the pending surgical treatment and radiotherapy. A potential inconsistency: the Local Committee report describes a March 2023 staging CT that 'documented metastatic disease,' yet the primary staging is described as M0 — this internal ambiguity exists in the document itself, not introduced by the N/O columns.

rows.N: Metastatic clear cell renal cancer stage IV

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, metastatic, Stage Four
fields.PATIENT_AGE: NOT FOUND (age not explicitly stated in the document)
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Male patient, patient at Hospital San Vicente de Paul. Diagnosed with metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma Stage Four. The treating oncologist (Dr. Natalia Jimenez Brenes) requested Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 21 days and Axitinib 5 mg twice daily by mouth for 6 months, in combination. The decision was made jointly with an interdisciplinary team. The patient was reported to be in good functional condition, autonomous for daily activities, with ECOG Performance Status 0. The Local Pharmacotherapeutics Committee forwarded the request to the Central Pharmacotherapeutics Committee on 07 October 2022. The Central Committee denied the request by note GM-CCF-3661-2022 of 27 October 2022 (session 2022-42, 19 October 2022), citing preliminary study results, uncertainty about long-term benefit, and availability of sunitinib as an institutional alternative.
fields.SYMPTOMS: NOT FOUND (no specific symptoms listed in the document beyond the oncological diagnosis)
fields.DISEASE_HISTORY: Patient diagnosed with metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma Stage Four. The treating oncologist submitted a non-LOM chronic treatment request on 03 October 2022 for the combination of Pembrolizumab and Axitinib based on the KEYNOTE-426 study. The Local Committee forwarded the request on 07 October 2022. On 27 October 2022 the Central Pharmacotherapeutics Committee (session 2022-42) denied the request citing: preliminary results of KEYNOTE-426 (study completion estimated December 2023), uncertainty about long-term survival benefit, recommendation by NICE against pembrolizumab-axitinib in previously untreated advanced renal carcinoma, and availability of sunitinib institutionally. The amparo was filed on 31 October 2022. The Constitutional Chamber requested a forensic medical opinion. On 28 November 2022, the forensic expert (Dictamen Medico Forense No. 2022-0008435) concluded that the combination of Pembrolizumab and Axitinib is the most beneficial option for the patient, conferring overall survival benefit and response rates with acceptable tolerability, as demonstrated in KEYNOTE-426, and recommended by European and North American guidelines.
fields.FUNCTIONAL_LIMITATIONS: ECOG Performance Status 0 (patient is autonomous for daily activities, in good functional condition)
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: NOT FOUND (no prior oncological treatments documented in the record; the request was for first-line treatment)
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND (Pembrolizumab plus Axitinib was requested as first-line treatment; no prior treatment failure documented)
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 %: NOT FOUND | Microsatellite Instability status: NOT FOUND | Tumor Mutational Burden: NOT FOUND | Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2: NOT FOUND | Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor: NOT FOUND | Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase: NOT FOUND | ROS1: NOT FOUND | KRAS: NOT FOUND | B-Raf Proto-Oncogene: NOT FOUND | others: NOT FOUND
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: NOT FOUND (metastatic disease confirmed but specific metastatic sites not described in this document)
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: The treating oncologist and the interdisciplinary team at Hospital San Vicente de Paul requested Pembrolizumab plus Axitinib based on KEYNOTE-426, which demonstrated overall survival benefit (hazard ratio 0.68, p=0.0003) and improved progression-free survival (15.4 months vs 11.1 months) compared to sunitinib for advanced clear cell renal carcinoma as first-line treatment. The oncology service stated the patient meets criteria for overall survival prolongation, prolonged recurrence-free survival, and higher response rates with Axitinib plus Pembrolizumab. The forensic medical expert confirmed that the combination is indicated, provides overall survival benefit with tolerable safety, is recommended in European and North American guidelines, and is advantageous compared to sunitinib.
fields.URGENCY: NOT FOUND (urgency not explicitly stated; the case was filed as an amparo after institutional denial)
fields.RISK_DEATH: Metastatic Stage Four renal cancer — inherently life-threatening; the treating physician and forensic expert emphasized overall survival benefit of the requested treatment
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab 200 mg intravenously every 21 days combined with Axitinib 5 mg by mouth twice daily, for 6 months
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Constitutional Chamber noted that when a conflict exists between the treating physician and the Central Pharmacotherapeutics Committee regarding non-LOM medications, this court has consistently respected the treating physician's criterion. To resolve the technical dispute, the court sought an independent expert opinion from the Forensic Medicine Department of the OIJ (Dictamen Medico Forense No. 2022-0008435), which concluded that the combination of Pembrolizumab and Axitinib is the most beneficial option for the patient, with overall survival benefit, higher response rates, and tolerable side effects compared to sunitinib. Based on this impartial determination, the court found there was no contraindication to supplying the combination, and ordered the immediate provision of both drugs in the dose and duration prescribed by the treating physician, under her full responsibility.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Article 73 of the Constitucion Politica de Costa Rica (CCSS autonomy); Articles 10 and 48 of the Constitucion Politica (Constitutional Chamber jurisdiction); Article 71 of the Ley de la Jurisdiccion Constitucional (penalty for non-compliance); KEYNOTE-426 study (Powles et al. / Bedke et al., European Urology 2022, NCT02853331); NICE guidance TA650 on pembrolizumab with axitinib for untreated advanced renal cell carcinoma; Dictamen Medico Forense No. 2022-0008435 (Randall Zuniga Perez, OIJ, 28 November 2022); Constitutional Chamber jurisprudence on treating physician's criterion in non-LOM drug conflicts; Reglamento sobre Expediente Electronico ante el Poder Judicial (Corte Plena session No. 27-11, 22 August 2011, article XXVI); Consejo Superior del Poder Judicial session No. 43-12, 3 May 2012, article LXXXI; CCSS circular norms on non-LOM medications (Circulars 6599/1999, 6263/2003, 22375/2005, 24296/2005, 41171/2005, DMT 582-11-05, 36385/2006, GDOP-27125-2007, 54480-A-1/2007, 53265-4/2008, CCF-1229-08-08/2008)

rows.N: Kidney cancer with metastasis to lungs, lymph nodes and skin

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, metastatic to lungs, retroperitoneal lymph nodes, and skin
fields.PATIENT_AGE: NOT FOUND (age not explicitly stated in the document)
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Female patient, treated at Hospital San Vicente de Paul. Diagnosed with clear cell renal cell carcinoma metastatic to lungs, retroperitoneal lymph nodes, and skin. In June 2022, while the patient was in good functional condition, the treating oncologist (Dr. Maria Jose Ibarra Valdeperas) decided to request the combination of Pembrolizumab and Axitinib (non-LOM drugs), in accordance with international evidence and guidelines. The Oncology Service noted that pain was controlled with morphine prescribed by the Palliative Care service at Hospital San Vicente de Paul, and the patient was independent for her basic daily activities. The patient was adherent to her scheduled appointments and prescribed treatments. She continued in the same clinical scenario as when the non-LOM drug combination was requested.
fields.SYMPTOMS: Pain (controlled with morphine prescribed by Palliative Care service); metastatic disease to lungs, retroperitoneal lymph nodes, and skin
fields.DISEASE_HISTORY: The patient was diagnosed with clear cell renal cell carcinoma with metastases to lungs, retroperitoneal lymph nodes, and skin. In June 2022, the treating oncologist submitted a non-LOM chronic treatment request for Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks for six months and Axitinib 5 mg twice daily by mouth. The Local Pharmacotherapeutics Committee forwarded the request on 30 June 2022. The Central Pharmacotherapeutics Committee, in session 2022-27 on 06 July 2022, evaluated the case and denied the request by note GM-CCF-E-0636-2022 (14 July 2022), citing that the clinical studies had not concluded, the use was potentially experimental, uncertainty about clinical benefit, NICE not recommending the combination, and availability of sunitinib institutionally. The amparo was filed on 05 August 2022. The Constitutional Chamber sought a forensic medical opinion (Dictamen Medico Legal No. 2022-0005816, 19 August 2022), which concluded that the combination of Pembrolizumab plus Axitinib is indicated and more beneficial in the patient's current clinical context.
fields.FUNCTIONAL_LIMITATIONS: Patient is in good functional condition; independent for basic daily activities; pain controlled with morphine
fields.COMORBIDITIES: NOT FOUND (no additional comorbidities documented beyond the metastatic cancer and palliative pain management)
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Palliative care with morphine for pain control; no prior systemic oncological treatment documented in the record for the renal cancer
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND (Pembrolizumab plus Axitinib was the first-line systemic oncological treatment requested; no prior oncological drug failure documented)
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 %: NOT FOUND | Microsatellite Instability status: NOT FOUND | Tumor Mutational Burden: NOT FOUND | Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2: NOT FOUND | Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor: NOT FOUND | Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase: NOT FOUND | ROS1: NOT FOUND | KRAS: NOT FOUND | B-Raf Proto-Oncogene: NOT FOUND | others: NOT FOUND
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: The treating oncologist stated that the combination of Pembrolizumab plus Axitinib is the only treatment option that would benefit the patient, in accordance with international evidence and guidelines. The forensic medical expert (Dictamen Medico Legal No. 2022-0005816) concluded that the combined therapy is indicated and more beneficial in the patient's current clinical context, requiring strict follow-up due to the possibility of side effects.
fields.URGENCY: NOT FOUND (urgency not explicitly stated; the amparo was filed after institutional denial)
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: NOT FOUND (not explicitly stated beyond the underlying metastatic disease)
fields.RISK_DEATH: Metastatic renal cell carcinoma — inherently life-threatening; the court noted the constitutional right to health cannot yield to economic criteria and the treating physician affirmed the treatment was the only beneficial option
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab 200 mg intravenously every 3 weeks for six months combined with Axitinib 5 mg by mouth twice daily
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Constitutional Chamber reiterated its consistent jurisprudence of respecting the treating physician's criterion when there is a conflict with the Central Pharmacotherapeutics Committee over non-LOM medications. To resolve the technical dispute, the court sought an independent forensic medical opinion (Dictamen Medico Legal No. 2022-0005816), which concluded that the combination of Pembrolizumab plus Axitinib is indicated and more beneficial for this patient in her current clinical context. Based on this impartial expert determination — which endorsed the treating physician's criterion — the court ordered the immediate provision of both drugs in the dose, duration, and timing prescribed by the treating physician, under her full responsibility.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Articles 10 and 48 of the Constitucion Politica de Costa Rica (Constitutional Chamber jurisdiction to protect fundamental rights); Article 73 of the Constitucion Politica (CCSS autonomy); Article 71 of the Ley de la Jurisdiccion Constitucional (penalty for non-compliance with amparo orders); Dictamen Medico Legal No. 2022-0005816 (OIJ, 19 August 2022); KEYNOTE-426 study (referenced by institutional reports); NICE guidance on pembrolizumab with axitinib; Constitutional Chamber jurisprudence on treating physician's criterion in non-LOM drug conflicts; Reglamento sobre Expediente Electronico ante el Poder Judicial (Corte Plena session No. 27-11, 22 August 2011, article XXVI); Consejo Superior del Poder Judicial session No. 43-12, 3 May 2012, article LXXXI

missing_from_no: Patient age is absent. The specific metastatic sites (retroperitoneal lymph nodes and skin, in addition to lungs) are only partially captured in column N (which mentions 'lymph nodes' generically without specifying retroperitoneal). The palliative care context (morphine for pain) is not captured in N/O. The forensic expert finding and judicial decision outcome are absent. Column O's description of 'already in palliative care' is partially correct but may be misleading (see below).
possibly_incorrect: Column O states 'Already in palliative care' — the document confirms the patient was receiving palliative care pain management with morphine from the Palliative Care service; however, the treating oncologist described the patient as being in good functional condition and independent for daily activities, and the requested treatment was active systemic therapy (Pembrolizumab plus Axitinib) rather than palliative-only care. The description 'already in palliative care' is factually correct regarding pain management but could be misread as implying the patient was receiving best supportive care only, which is not the full picture. No other conflicts detected.

rows.N: Squamous cell carcinoma of the oral cavity with pulmonary metastasis
rows.O: PD-L1 80%. The patient attempted first-line chemotherapy but could not tolerate the treatment due to toxicity, leaving few conventional therapeutic alternatives.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Squamous cell carcinoma of the oral cavity (buccal cavity), metastatic to the lung
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Female patient, 67 years old, known hypothyroid and asthmatic. She was diagnosed in December 2020 with squamous cell carcinoma of the oral cavity metastatic to the lung. She received first-line chemotherapy from April 2020 to June 2020 (4 applications), after which surveillance was maintained. In January 2021, the disease in the oral cavity showed progression (growth). A Programmed Death-Ligand 1 expression study showed 80%. In January 2022, a new assessment found the primary lesion in the oral cavity had grown significantly and in an accelerated manner. The treating oncologist (Dr. Cinthya Monge Castro, Hospital San Juan de Dios) recommended treatment with Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks for 6 months, based on a Phase III open-label randomized trial demonstrating the benefit of Pembrolizumab with or without chemotherapy in recurrent or metastatic head and neck cancer versus standard treatment. The cost of the treatment was documented: unit cost 2,701,989 colones; monthly cost 5,403,978 colones; total 6-month cost 43,231,824 colones.
fields.SYMPTOMS: Progressive growth of primary oral cavity lesion (accelerated growth noted in January 2022); pulmonary metastasis; functional deterioration associated with prior chemotherapy
fields.DISEASE_HISTORY: Diagnosed December 2020 with oral cavity squamous cell carcinoma metastatic to the lung. First-line chemotherapy (4 cycles, April to June 2020) was suspended because the patient's body could not tolerate it well and she experienced significant functional deterioration. Surveillance from June 2020 onwards. In January 2021, oral cavity disease showed progression. Programmed Death-Ligand 1 expression measured at 80%. In January 2022, accelerated progression of the primary oral lesion was documented. The Oncology Session at Hospital San Juan de Dios on 10 January 2022 recommended Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks for 6 months. The Local Pharmacotherapeutics Committee submitted the request to the Central Committee on 17 January 2022. The Central Committee, in session 2022-04 on 26 January 2022 (note GM-CCF-0553-2022, 03 February 2022), placed the case under analysis. On 02 March 2022 (session 2022-09), the Central Committee denied the request by note GM-CCF-1153-2022 (10 March 2022), citing limited evidence from KEYNOTE-40 (5.0 months overall survival difference in Programmed Death-Ligand 1 greater than or equal to 50% subgroup only, with overlapping confidence intervals; ESMO classification 3/5 vs EXTREME standard), insufficient objective parameters to support the request. The amparo was filed on 02 March 2022. The Constitutional Chamber ordered a forensic medical evaluation; the Forensic Medical Section (Grettchen Flores Sandi and Sandra Solorzano Herra) assessed the patient on 21 March 2022 and concluded that Pembrolizumab is indicated and more beneficial in her current clinical context (Dictamen Medico Legal, 24 March 2022).
fields.FUNCTIONAL_LIMITATIONS: Significant functional deterioration experienced during first-line chemotherapy (treatment had to be suspended); specific functional status at time of amparo not documented beyond the history of prior deterioration
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: First-line chemotherapy (4 cycles, April to June 2020) — suspended due to intolerance and significant functional deterioration
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: First-line chemotherapy was discontinued due to toxicity/intolerance (patient's body could not tolerate it well, causing significant functional deterioration); disease progression in the oral cavity was documented in January 2021 and January 2022
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 %: 80% | Microsatellite Instability status: NOT FOUND | Tumor Mutational Burden: NOT FOUND | Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2: NOT FOUND | Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor: NOT FOUND | Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase: NOT FOUND | ROS1: NOT FOUND | KRAS: NOT FOUND | B-Raf Proto-Oncogene: NOT FOUND | others: NOT FOUND
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: The treating oncologist (Dr. Cinthya Monge Castro) based the request on a Phase III open-label randomized trial showing Pembrolizumab (with or without chemotherapy) is superior in overall survival compared to the standard cetuximab-plus-chemotherapy regimen, particularly in patients with Programmed Death-Ligand 1 greater than 20%; Pembrolizumab monotherapy is also superior with fewer adverse effects. This is a Category 1 recommendation in NCCN guidelines for recurrent or metastatic head and neck cancer, and Pembrolizumab is FDA-approved for metastatic head and neck squamous cell carcinoma. The forensic medical expert confirmed that Pembrolizumab is indicated and more beneficial for this patient, noting that the dose for head and neck squamous cell carcinoma is 200 mg intravenously every 30 minutes every 3 weeks until disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, or up to 24 months.
fields.URGENCY: High — the primary lesion had grown significantly and in an accelerated manner in January 2022; limited conventional therapeutic alternatives due to prior chemotherapy intolerance
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: High risk of rapid deterioration given accelerated disease progression and the patient's prior inability to tolerate standard chemotherapy, leaving few conventional alternatives
fields.RISK_DEATH: Metastatic squamous cell carcinoma with accelerated progression and prior chemotherapy failure — life-threatening; the court found the denial jeopardized the patient's right to health and life
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab 200 mg intravenously every 3 weeks for 6 months
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Constitutional Chamber noted that the Gerente Medico and Coordinador del Comite Central de Farmacoterapia of the CCSS failed to submit the ordered report, and therefore, pursuant to Article 45 of the Ley de la Jurisdiccion Constitucional, the facts attributable to those officials were taken as established. The court obtained an independent forensic medical opinion from the Forensic Medical Section of the OIJ (assessed 21 March 2022), which concluded that Pembrolizumab is indicated and more beneficial for this patient in her current clinical context, consistent with the treating physician's recommendation. Based on this impartial expert determination, the court ordered the immediate provision of Pembrolizumab in the dose, conditions, and duration prescribed by the treating physician, under her full responsibility and supervision.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Article 45 of the Ley de la Jurisdiccion Constitucional (facts deemed proven when recurridos fail to report); Article 71 of the Ley de la Jurisdiccion Constitucional (penalty for non-compliance with amparo orders); KEYNOTE-40 study (referenced in Central Committee decision — note: the treating physician cited a Phase III trial consistent with KEYNOTE-048 or similar; the committee cited KEYNOTE-40 which evaluates second-line use); NCCN guidelines Category 1 recommendation for Pembrolizumab in recurrent or metastatic head and neck cancer; ESMO classification for the EXTREME regimen; FDA approval for metastatic head and neck squamous cell carcinoma; Dictamen Medico Legal (Grettchen Flores Sandi and Sandra Solorzano Herra, OIJ, 24 March 2022); Constitutional Chamber jurisprudence on treating physician's criterion; Reglamento sobre Expediente Electronico ante el Poder Judicial (Corte Plena session No. 27-11, 22 August 2011, article XXVI); Consejo Superior del Poder Judicial session No. 43-12, 3 May 2012, article LXXXI

missing_from_no: Row 165 (case 06935-2022) is correctly flagged as 'Not about pembrolizumab' and was not analyzed for clinical fields. For row 176 (case 06754-2022): patient age (67 years old) is absent from N/O. Comorbidities (hypothyroidism and asthma) are absent. The specific timing and nature of chemotherapy failure are more nuanced in the document than described in column O (the chemotherapy was from April to June 2020, with subsequent surveillance and progression; the Programmed Death-Ligand 1 test result preceded the Pembrolizumab request by approximately one year). The judicial decision outcome (granted), the ECOG Performance Status, and the Central Committee's failure to submit its report (leading to deemed facts) are absent from N/O.
possibly_incorrect: Row 165: the document does not contain a separate case 06935-2022; the document is entirely about case 06754-2022. The label 'Not about pembrolizumab' for row 165 is incorrect — the document is entirely about Pembrolizumab. This may reflect a row-numbering mismatch in the source dataset rather than a content error. Row 176: Column O states 'the patient attempted first-line chemotherapy but could not tolerate the treatment due to toxicity' — the document confirms this (the treatment was suspended after 4 cycles due to intolerance and significant functional deterioration). Column O also states Programmed Death-Ligand 1 80%, which is confirmed in the document. No factual conflicts detected for row 176.

rows.N: Small cell lung cancer stage IVB with mediastinal lymph node metastasis

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Small cell lung cancer, Stage Four B, with mediastinal lymph node metastasis
fields.PATIENT_AGE: 74 years old (adult older person, confirmed via Electoral Tribunal records)
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Male patient, 74 years old, patient at the Oncology Service of Hospital San Vicente de Paul. Diagnosed with small cell lung cancer Stage Four B with mediastinal lymph node metastasis. His treating oncologist (Dr. Natalia Jimenez) requested Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV once every 3 weeks for 6 months on 14 September 2021. The Local Pharmacotherapeutics Committee elevated the case to the Central Pharmacotherapeutics Committee on 16 September 2021. The Central Committee requested additional clinical history and scientific evidence on 21 September 2021 (note GM-CCF-3638-2021). The treating physician responded on 22 September 2021 (note HSVP-SOM-66-2021). In session No. 2021-38 on 29 September 2021, the Central Committee denied the request (note GM-CCF-3933-2021, 07 October 2021), stating the evidence showed only marginal clinical benefit (0.2 months improvement in progression-free survival and 1.1 months in overall survival over placebo), high risk of severe adverse effects, and that the treating physician had not provided evidence of efficacy and safety for this condition in the initial submission. At the time of the decision, the patient was described as having locally advanced disease, with palliative management in place.
fields.SYMPTOMS: NOT FOUND (specific symptoms not listed in the document; the patient was described as being in excellent functional condition at the time of the treatment request)
fields.DISEASE_HISTORY: Patient diagnosed with small cell lung cancer Stage Four B with mediastinal lymph node metastasis. On 14 September 2021 the treating oncologist submitted a non-LOM chronic treatment request for Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks for 6 months. The Oncology Service coordinator noted the patient meets criteria for benefit with Pembrolizumab and is in excellent functional condition to begin treatment. The Central Pharmacotherapeutics Committee denied the request in session 2021-38 (29 September 2021), citing marginal survival benefits (0.2 months in progression-free survival; 1.1 months in overall survival over placebo), high adverse effect risk, and lack of clinical significance. The amparo was filed on 17 October 2021. The Constitutional Chamber requested a forensic medical opinion (Dictamen Medico Legal No. 2021-0008249, received 05 November 2021) from the Judicial Investigation Agency (OIJ), which concluded that 'with the elements of judgment available at the time of issuing this opinion, including the interconsultation with the oncology specialist, the addition of Pembrolizumab is not justified in this patient.'
fields.FUNCTIONAL_LIMITATIONS: Patient described as being in excellent functional condition (noted by the Oncology Service at the time of treatment request); palliative management in place at time of amparo decision
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Palliative care management (best supportive care provided by the institution); specific prior oncological treatments not detailed in the document
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND (the document indicates the treating physician did not fully provide evidence of efficacy and safety for this indication; the Central Committee noted absence of supporting evidence in the initial submission; prior treatment failure not documented)
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 %: NOT FOUND | Microsatellite Instability status: NOT FOUND | Tumor Mutational Burden: NOT FOUND | Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2: NOT FOUND | Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor: NOT FOUND | Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase: NOT FOUND | ROS1: NOT FOUND | KRAS: NOT FOUND | B-Raf Proto-Oncogene: NOT FOUND | others: NOT FOUND
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: The treating oncologist (Dr. Natalia Jimenez) and the Oncology Service coordinator stated that the patient meets criteria for benefit with Pembrolizumab, referencing scientific evidence of overall survival and progression-free survival improvement compared to placebo, and that the patient is in excellent functional condition for treatment. However, the Central Pharmacotherapeutics Committee countered that the survival benefit is clinically insignificant (0.2 months in progression-free survival; 1.1 months in overall survival), the scheme is not the highest standard for this case, adverse effects are significant, and the evidence did not establish a real benefit expectation. The institutional report noted the patient receives the best available palliative management.
fields.URGENCY: NOT FOUND (urgency not explicitly stated; the treating oncologist noted the patient was in excellent functional condition)
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: NOT FOUND (not explicitly stated beyond the locally advanced metastatic disease context)
fields.RISK_DEATH: Small cell lung cancer Stage Four B — inherently life-threatening; however, the forensic expert concluded Pembrolizumab was not justified, and the court upheld the denial
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab 200 mg intravenously once every 3 weeks for 6 months
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Constitutional Chamber applied its jurisprudence that when there is a conflict between the treating physician and the Central Pharmacotherapeutics Committee, an independent forensic medical opinion is sought to resolve the technical dispute. The forensic expert (Dictamen Medico Legal No. 2021-0008249, OIJ) concluded that, based on available evidence and an oncology specialist consultation, 'the addition of Pembrolizumab is not justified in this patient.' The court relied on this impartial determination by the OIJ — the same third-party mechanism used in cases where the court grants the amparo — and concluded that Pembrolizumab is not the most recommended therapy for this patient at this time. The court ordered the CCSS to continue providing the patient with the medical attention required for his condition using institutional medication alternatives.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Constitutional Chamber Voto No. 2014-001072 (14:30 hours, 28 January 2014 — on treating physician criterion and non-LOM drug conflicts); Constitutional Chamber sentence No. 2009-08694 (16:11 hours, 26 May 2009 — on right to health prevailing over economic criteria); Constitutional Chamber sentence No. 2004-003363 (31 March 2004) and No. 2007-003018 (15:29 hours, 6 March 2007 — on respecting the treating physician's prescription freedom); Dictamen Medico Legal No. 2021-0008249 (OIJ Forensic Medicine Department, received 05 November 2021); Article 71 of the Ley de la Jurisdiccion Constitucional; Reglamento sobre Expediente Electronico ante el Poder Judicial (Corte Plena session No. 27-11, 22 August 2011, article XXVI); Consejo Superior del Poder Judicial session No. 43-12, 3 May 2012, article LXXXI

missing_from_no: Patient age (74 years old) is absent from N/O. The judicial decision outcome (denied — the amparo was not granted) is not reflected in N/O, which is a critical omission given this is the only case in the batch where the request for Pembrolizumab was denied. The forensic expert conclusion against Pembrolizumab and the Central Committee's detailed reasoning (marginal survival benefit of 0.2 months progression-free survival and 1.1 months overall survival) are absent. ECOG Performance Status (excellent functional condition) is absent.
possibly_incorrect: Column N states 'Small cell lung cancer stage IVB with mediastinal lymph node metastasis' — the document confirms 'cancer de pulmon de celulas pequenas EC IVB por metastasis ganglionar mediastinal,' which matches precisely. Column O states 'Palliative treatment' — the document confirms palliative management was in place; however, the treating oncologist had specifically requested Pembrolizumab as an active treatment, not as palliative care. The description 'palliative treatment' could be interpreted as describing the patient's institutional management context rather than the treatment requested, which may be slightly misleading. No factual conflicts detected.
